sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: ὦ γῆς παλαιὸν ἄργος , Ἰνάχου ῥοαί ,
sections.translation: O ancient plain of land , the streams of Inachus , from which king Agamemnon once mounted war on a thousand ships and sailed to the land of Troy . After he had slain Priam , the ruler of Ilium ,

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: Πρίαμον , ἑλών τε Δαρδάνου κλεινὴν πόλιν ,
sections.translation: and captured the famous city of Dardanus , he came here to Argos and set up on the high temples many spoils of the barbarians . And in Troy he was successful ; but at home he died by the guile of his wife Clytemnestra

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: καὶ τοῦ Θυέστου παιδὸς Αἰγίσθου χερί .
sections.translation: and the hand of Aegisthus , son of Thyestes . And he left behind the ancient scepter of Tantalus , and is dead ; but Aegisthus rules the land , possessing Agamemnon ’ s wife , the daughter of Tyndareus . Now as for those whom he left in his house when he sailed to Troy ,

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: ἄρσενά τʼ Ὀρέστην θῆλύ τʼ Ἠλέκτρας θάλος ,
sections.translation: his son Orestes and his young daughter Electra : when Orestes was about to die at the hand of Aegisthus , his father ’ s old servant stole him away and gave him to Strophius to bring up in the land of the Phocians . Electra stayed in her father ’ s house ,

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: ταύτην ἐπειδὴ θαλερὸς εἶχʼ ἥβης χρόνος ,
sections.translation: and when she came to the blooming season of youth , the foremost suitors of the land of Hellas asked for her in marriage . But Aegisthus feared she might bear to some chieftain a son who would avenge Agamemnon , and so he kept her at home and did not betroth her to any bridegroom .

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δὲ καὶ τοῦτʼ ἦν φόβου πολλοῦ πλέων ,
sections.translation: When even this filled him with great fear , that she might secretly bear children to some noble lord , Aegisthus planned to kill her , but her mother , although cruel at heart , rescued her from his hand . For she had a pretext for having slain her husband ,

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: παίδων δʼ ἔδεισε μὴ φθονηθείη φόνῳ .
sections.translation: but she feared that she would be despised for the murder of her children . So then , for these reasons , Aegisthus devised such a scheme : he promised gold to anyone who should kill Agamemnon ’ s son , who had left the country as an exile , while Electra he gave

sections.label: Section 35
sections.translation: in marriage to me . My ancestors were Mycenaeans ; in that respect at least I am not to blame . My family was noble in race but poor in money — which is the ruin of good birth . He gave her to a powerless man so that his fear might lose its power .

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: εἰ γάρ νιν ἔσχεν ἀξίωμʼ ἔχων ἀνήρ ,
sections.translation: For if some man of high position got her , he would have roused the sleeping blood of Agamemnon and judgment would have come at some time to Aegisthus . But I have never ( Cypris knows this too ) dishonored her in bed ; she is still a virgin indeed .

sections.label: Section 45
sections.translation: I am ashamed to have the daughter of a wealthy man and violate her , when I was not born of equal rank . And I groan for the wretched Orestes , called my kinsman , if he shall ever return to Argos and see the unfortunate marriage of his sister .

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: ὅστις δέ μʼ εἶναί φησι μῶρον , εἰ λαβὼν
sections.translation: And whoever says I am a fool if I do not touch a young girl when I have her in my house , let him know that he measures soundness of mind by worthless standards of judgment , and he himself is a fool .

sections.label: Section 54
sections.original: ὦ νὺξ μέλαινα , χρυσέων ἄστρων τροφέ ,
sections.translation: O black night , nurse of the golden stars ,

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: ἐν ᾗ τόδʼ ἄγγος τῷδʼ ἐφεδρεῦον κάρᾳ
sections.translation: in which I go to the river ’ s streams , bearing this pitcher resting on my head — not because I have come to such a point of necessity , but so that I may show to the gods Aegisthus ’ insolence — and send forth laments into the wide sky , to my father .

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: ἡ γὰρ πανώλης Τυνδαρίς , μήτηρ ἐμή ,
sections.translation: For the deadly daughter of Tyndareus , my mother , has cast me out of the house to please her husband ; since she has borne other children in her union with Aegisthus , she considers Orestes and me secondary in the home .

sections.label: Section 64
sections.original: τί γὰρ τάδʼ , ὦ δύστηνʼ , ἐμὴν μοχθεῖς χάριν
sections.translation: Why , unhappy one , do you do this work , laboring for my sake ,

sections.label: Section 65
sections.translation: though you were well brought up before , and do not stop , even when I tell you this ?

sections.label: Section 67
sections.original: ἐγώ σʼ ἴσον θεοῖσιν ἡγοῦμαι φίλον ·
sections.translation: I hold you equal to the gods in kindness , for in my distress you have not insulted me . It is a great allotment for mortals

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: ἰατρὸν εὑρεῖν , ὡς ἐγὼ σὲ λαμβάνω .
sections.translation: to find a healer for ill fortune , such as I have in you . And so , even though unbidden , I ought to share your labors , relieving you of work as far as I have strength , so that you may bear it more easily . You have enough to do outside ; I must keep the house

sections.label: Section 75
sections.translation: in order . It is pleasant for the worker coming in from outside to find things within right .

sections.label: Section 77
sections.original: εἴ τοι δοκεῖ σοι , στεῖχε · καὶ γὰρ οὐ πρόσω
sections.translation: Go , then , if you wish ; and in fact the springs are not far from my house . When it is day , I will drive the oxen to my lands and sow the fields .

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: ἀργὸς γὰρ οὐδεὶς θεοὺς ἔχων ἀνὰ στόμα
sections.translation: For no idler , though he has the gods ’ names always on his lips , can gather a livelihood without hard work . Exeunt Peasant and Electra . Enter Orestes and Pylades .

sections.label: Section 82
sections.original: Πυλάδη , σὲ γὰρ δὴ πρῶτον ἀνθρώπων ἐγὼ
sections.translation: Pylades , I hold you first among men as a kind and trusted friend to me . You alone of my friends have honored me , Orestes ,

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: πράσσονθʼ ἃ πράσσω δείνʼ ὑπʼ Αἰγίσθου παθών ,
sections.translation: being as I am in dreadful suffering from Aegisthus , who killed my father , he and my most deadly mother . I have come from the mystic shrine of the god to Argive land , and no one knows it , to repay my father ’ s murderers with murder .

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: νυκτὸς δὲ τῆσδε πρὸς τάφον μολὼν πατρὸς
sections.translation: During this past night , going to my father ’ s tomb , I wept and cut off a lock of my hair as an offering and sacrificed over the altar the blood of a slaughtered sheep , unnoticed by the tyrants who rule this land . And now I do not set foot within the walls ,

sections.label: Section 95
sections.translation: but I have come to the borders of this land combining two desires : I may escape to another country if anyone on the watch should recognize me ; and , looking for my sister ( for they say that she lives here , joined in marriage , and is no longer a virgin ),

sections.label: Section 100
sections.translation: I may meet with her and , having her as an accomplice for murder , I may learn clearly what is happening within the walls . And now , since dawn is lifting up her bright eye , let us step aside from this path . For either some plowman or serving maid

sections.label: Section 105
sections.translation: will come in our sight , from whom we may ask if my sister lives in this place . But now that I see this maidservant , bearing a weight of water on her shorn head , let us sit down , and inquire

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original: δούλης γυναικός , ἤν τι δεξώμεσθʼ ἔπος
sections.translation: of this slave girl , if we may receive some word about the matter , Pylades , for which we have come to this land . They retire a little .

sections.label: Section 1.112
sections.translation: Hasten your step , it is time ; go onward , onward , weeping ! Ah me !

sections.label: Section 1.115
sections.translation: I am Agamemnon ’ s child , and Clytemnestra , hated daughter of Tyndareus , bore me ; the citizens call me unhappy Electra .

sections.label: Section 1.120
sections.translation: Alas for my cruel pain and hateful life ! O father , Agamemnon , you lie in Hades , by the butchery of your wife and Aegisthus .

sections.label: Section 125
sections.translation: Come , waken the same lament , take up the enjoyment of long weeping .

sections.label: Section 1.127
sections.translation: Hasten your step , it is time ; go onward , onward , weeping . Ah me !

sections.label: Section 1.130
sections.original: τίνα πόλιν , τίνα δʼ οἶκον , ὦ
sections.translation: In what city and what household do you wander about , my wretched brother , leaving your pitiable sister in our ancestral home , to great pain ?

sections.label: Section 1.135
sections.translation: Come to me , the unhappy one , as a deliverer from this pain , oh Zeus , Zeus , and as a defender for my father against his most hateful bloodshed ; bring the wanderer to shore in Argos .

sections.label: Section 2.140
sections.original: θὲς τόδε τεῦχος ἐμῆς ἀπὸ κρατὸς ἑ-
sections.translation: Take this pitcher from my head and put it down , so that I may cry aloud the night-time laments for my father . A wail , a song of death , of death , for you , father , under the earth , I speak the laments

sections.label: Section 2.145
sections.translation: in which I am always engaged , day by day , tearing my skin with my nails , and striking my cropped head with my hand , for your death .

sections.label: Section 150
sections.translation: Oh , oh , tear my face ; as a clear-sounding swan beside the river ’ s streams calls to its dearest father , dying in the crafty snares of the

sections.label: Section 155
sections.translation: net , so I lament you , my unhappy father ,

sections.label: Section 2.157
sections.translation: washed by the very last bath , in the most piteous bed of death . Oh , me ,

sections.label: Section 2.160
sections.translation: your bitter cleaving by the axe , father , the bitter plans of the way from Troy ! Your wife welcomed you with no victor ’ s garlands or crowns , but with a two-edged sword ,

sections.label: Section 2.165
sections.translation: making you the mournful victim of Aigisthus , she got a treacherous bed-fellow .

sections.label: Section 3.167
sections.translation: O Electra , daughter of Agamemnon , I have come to your rustic courtyard . A milk-drinker from Mycenae has come , he has come ,

sections.label: Section 3.170
sections.translation: a mountain walker ; he reports that the Argives are proclaiming a sacrifice for the third day from now , and that all maidens are to go to Hera ’ s temple .

sections.label: Section 3.175
sections.translation: My unhappy heart beats fast , friends , but not at adornment or gold ; nor will I set up choruses with the maidens of Argos

sections.label: Section 3.180
sections.translation: and beat my foot in the mazes of the dance . By tears I pass the night ; tears are my unhappy care day by day . See if my filthy hair ,

sections.label: Section 3.185
sections.translation: and the rags of my dress , will be fit for a princess , a daughter of Agamemnon , or for Troy , once taken , which remembers my father .

sections.label: Section 3.190
sections.translation: Mighty is the goddess ; come then , and borrow from me thick-woven clothes to wear , and gold — as a favor to me — accessories to adornment . Do you think to rule over your enemies by tears , if you do not revere the gods ?

sections.label: Section 3.195
sections.translation: Honoring the gods not by lamentation but by prayers , you will have good fortune , child .

sections.label: Section 3.198
sections.translation: No god attends to the voice of the ill-fated one ,

sections.label: Section 3.200
sections.translation: or to the slaying of my father long ago . Alas for the dead , and for the living vagabond , who dwells in another land somewhere ,

sections.label: Section 3.205
sections.translation: miserably wandering to a slave ’ s hearth , yet born of that renowned father . I myself live in a poor man ’ s house , wasting my life away , an exile from my father ’ s house ,

sections.label: Section 3.210
sections.translation: on the mountain crags . But my mother , with a new husband , makes her home in a bed stained by blood .

sections.label: Section 213
sections.translation: Helen , your mother ’ s sister , is the cause of many evils to the Hellenes and to your house .

sections.label: Section 215
sections.translation: Ah ! Women , I have broken off my lament ; strangers , who had their lair at the altar , are rising from ambush towards the household . Let us escape the villains by flight , you along the path and I to the house .

sections.label: Section 220
sections.original: μένʼ , ὦ τάλαινα · μὴ τρέσῃς ἐμὴν χέρα .
sections.translation: Stay , poor girl ; do not fear my hand .

sections.label: Section 221
sections.original: ὦ Φοῖβʼ Ἄπολλον · προσπίτνω σε μὴ θανεῖν .
sections.translation: O Phoebus Apollo ! I beseech you to spare my life .

sections.label: Section 222
sections.translation: May I kill others more hated than you !

sections.label: Section 223
sections.original: ἄπελθε , μὴ ψαῦʼ ὧν σε μὴ ψαύειν χρεών .
sections.translation: Go away ! Do not touch one whom you must not touch .

sections.label: Section 224
sections.original: οὐκ ἔσθʼ ὅτου θίγοιμʼ ἂν ἐνδικώτερον .
sections.translation: There is no one I have a better right to touch .
